artifacts.content:
From: Elena Rivera <elena.rivera@gmail.com>
To: Cactus View High School Front Office <office@cactusviewhs.org>
Subject: Update to Jordan Rivera Emergency & Contact Information
Date: 2025-01-22 08:14

Dear Front Office Staff,

I hope you’re doing well. I’m writing to update the contact and emergency information for my son, Jordan Michael Rivera (10th grade, Cactus View High School).

We recently realized that the phone number on file for me is our old landline, which we disconnected last year. Please update my primary contact number to my current cell: (602) 771-3054. I am Jordan’s primary parent/guardian and the best person to reach during the school day if there is any issue with his health, attendance, or behavior.

You should still have our home address listed as 4827 W Cactus Bloom Drive, Glendale, AZ 85304. That information is correct and has not changed. Jordan’s father, Carlos Rivera, is still the secondary contact and can be reached at his work number in Phoenix, but he’s often on shift and not always able to answer right away, which is why my cell is the most reliable.

If possible, please also note that Jordan has mild seasonal allergies and sometimes takes over-the-counter cetirizine in the mornings during high-pollen days. This usually doesn’t affect him, but I wanted to make sure his records are accurate.

Kindly confirm once the parent/guardian contact information has been updated in your system. Thank you for your help and for everything you do for the students.

Sincerely,

Elena Maria Rivera
Mother of Jordan M. Rivera
Cell: (602) 771-3054

From: Cactus View HS Attendance <attendance@cactusviewhs.org>
To: Elena Rivera <elena.rivera.az@gmail.com>
Cc: Jordan Rivera <jordan.rivera17@schoolmail.org>
Date: 2024-10-03 09:12 MST
Subject: Unable to reach parent/guardian by phone this morning

Dear Ms. Rivera,

This email is to inform you that we attempted to contact the parent/guardian listed for Jordan Rivera (ID: 209473) this morning regarding an unexcused first-period absence. The number on file, 623-880-4412, rang several times and then went to a generic voicemail that did not identify the Rivera family.

For student safety, it is important that we have an accurate parent/guardian phone number that can be reached during school hours. Please reply to this email or call the attendance office at 623-555-0100 to confirm the best number for daytime contact. If there is a separate emergency-only number (for example, a work line or a grandparent who is nearby), you may provide that as well.

Jordan, please note that if your parent/guardian contact information is not updated by the end of the month, a hold may be placed on certain privileges (off-campus passes, some extracurricular trips, etc.).

This notice is not a disciplinary action, but we do need accurate information. Also, Jordan mentioned seasonal allergies as a possible reason for feeling unwell; if there are any updated medical instructions, please share them with the nurse.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

Ms. Angela Ruiz
Attendance Office
Cactus View High School
attendance@cactusviewhs.org
Phone: 623-555-0100

From: Fry’s Food & Drug – Peoria Ave <store147@frysfood.com>
To: Jordan Rivera <jordan.rivera17@schoolmail.org>
Date: 2024-09-09 13:47 MST
Subject: Emergency Contact Form for Minor Employees

Hi Jordan,

Welcome again to Fry’s Store #147! Since you’re under 18, we need a completed Emergency Contact Form on file by the end of this week.

On the form, please list your primary parent/guardian contact. You wrote down a number that looks like 602-774-1305, but HR couldn’t confirm it when they tried a test call yesterday—it went to a disconnected line. Your parent/guardian may have changed numbers recently, or it might have just been hard to read.

Please double-check your mom or dad’s current phone and rewrite it clearly. If your mom prefers to be reached at a work line during the day (for example, a clinic or office number) and a cell in the evenings, you can list both. There’s space for a secondary emergency contact as well, such as a grandparent or uncle who lives nearby.

You can bring the corrected form to the customer service desk or scan and email it back to us. If it’s easier, have your parent/guardian call the store directly at 623-555-4720 and ask for Maria in HR so we can verify the number verbally.

Accurate contact info is important in case anything happens during your shift—especially since you mentioned mild allergies and the fact that you sometimes walk home after dark.

Thanks,

Maria Torres
HR Coordinator, Fry’s #147
store147@frysfood.com
Store phone: 623-555-4720

Hey Jordan,

Glad to hear you’re interested in joining the Glendale Youth Summer Basketball League again. Before I can finalize your spot, I need updated parent/guardian contact info for my roster and the city’s liability forms.

The number I have from last summer for your parent/guardian is **(623) 509-7721** under “Elena R.” I’m not sure if that’s your mom’s current cell or maybe a home line, so please confirm whether it’s still the best number to reach your parent or guardian during practices and games.

I also have a second number written down from your paperwork that looks like **(480) 300-1189**, but the handwriting is pretty bad, so I might have copied it wrong.

artifacts.content:
Subject: School Field Trip Medical & Emergency Form

Hi Mom,

I just got home and realized I forgot to give you the field trip packet from Cactus View. Mr. Nguyen is taking our history class to the Arizona Capitol Museum in Phoenix on March 28, and they need the signed permission slip, medical info, and emergency contact form turned in by this Friday.

I already filled in the easy parts (name, grade, your cell, etc.), but there’s a section where they ask who my primary emergency contact is and what their relationship is. I put Carlos Rivera for the name and wrote “Dad” for the relationship, since he’s usually the one who can leave work faster in an emergency and he’s the one listed on my last sports physical. They also asked for a secondary contact, so I listed you with your work number at the clinic.

There’s another page about medications and allergies. I marked that I sometimes take cetirizine for seasonal allergies and that I don’t have any serious food allergies, just mild issues with too much dairy. They also asked about health insurance, so I left that part blank for you to fill in because I don’t want to mess it up.

Can you look everything over, sign the bottom of both pages, and stick it back in my backpack tonight? I work my shift at Fry’s tomorrow from 5–9 p.m., so I want to turn it in during second period before I forget again.

Thanks,
Jordan

metadata.subject: School Field Trip Medical & Emergency Form

Hey mijo,

They gave examples like “mother,” “grandmother,” “aunt,” “older brother,” etc. Since I’m usually the one who takes you to Banner Thunderbird or the clinic if something happens, I put my name first. For the relationship field, I tentatively typed “mother/guardian,” but the form says you can choose anything that makes sense, like “parent,” “guardian,” or “mom.” You decide which looks better and update it before you submit.

Also, remember what Dr. Alvarez at Banner Thunderbird Medical Center told us back in March 2024 about your stomach issues? Because of your mild lactose sensitivity and that whole milkshake incident at Fry’s on W Peoria Ave when you had to leave work early, the coalition wants to know about any food stuff. artifacts.content:
Title: Fry’s – New Hire Safety & Emergency Orientation
Date: 2024-11-09 16:00-18:00
Location: Fry’s Food & Drug – W Peoria Ave, Break Room
Attendees: Jordan Rivera; Store Manager; HR Rep

Description:

Mandatory orientation for new cashiers covering safety procedures and emergency contacts.

Agenda:
- Store evacuation routes and fire drill procedures
- Who to notify first in case of injury (shift lead, manager on duty)
- Filling out the employee emergency contact card

Notes for Jordan:
- HR wants you to list a primary emergency contact who can be reached quickly in the evenings. They suggested a parent/guardian, but said you can also use a relative like a grandmother, aunt, or older cousin if that person usually answers the phone faster.
- On the form, there’s a box for “Relationship to Employee” with examples: “mother,” “guardian,” “grandmother,” “aunt,” “older brother,” etc. Decide which label to use for your main contact and double-check the spelling of their name.
- They also asked everyone to include any important medical/diet notes. You should mention your mild lactose sensitivity and that your pediatrician, Dr. Alvarez at Banner Thunderbird Medical Center, advised you not to have heavy cream-based foods at work. This is especially important after what happened when you got stomach cramps and diarrhea from that big milkshake on your dinner break and had to leave your shift two hours early.

Bring a pen and your contact list with phone numbers.

metadata.title: Fry’s – New Hire Safety & Emergency Orientation

artifacts.content:
From: Youth Coalition AZ <info@youthcoalitionaz.org>
To: Jordan Rivera <jordan.rivera@example.com>
Date: 2025-03-02 14:26
Subject: Incomplete Emergency Details – Action Required

Hi Jordan,

Thanks for registering for the Glendale Youth Coalition spring program. We reviewed your packet and noticed that the emergency contact section is incomplete.

You listed “Elena Maria Rivera” as the primary person to call, but the field asking for her relationship to you was left blank. For our records, we need a clear label such as “mother,” “parent/guardian,” or “legal guardian.” Please reply to this email with how you’d like that relationship to be identified, and we will update your file.

You also named “Rosa Delgado” as a backup contact but did not specify that she is your grandmother. If you want her to be authorized to pick you up from events, please confirm that we should list her relationship as “grandmother” on the sign-out sheet.

Finally, we need one clarification on your medical information. You wrote that you have mild lactose sensitivity and mentioned a past incident involving a large milkshake during a dinner break at Fry’s Food & Drug on W Peoria Ave. Our nurse would like you to confirm that your pediatrician, Dr. Alvarez at Banner Thunderbird Medical Center, recommended you avoid heavy cream-based foods (like Alfredo pasta or big ice cream servings) and stick to water or juice instead of milk during coalition events.

Reply with the relationship labels and confirmation of the dietary note by Friday.

Best,
Maya
Program Coordinator

metadata.subject: Incomplete Emergency Details – Action Required

artifacts.content:
From: Elena Rivera <elena.rivera@example.com>
To: youthcoordinator@glendalecoalition.org
Cc: Jordan Rivera <jordan.rivera09@example.com>
Date: 2025-01-08 18:42
Subject: Emergency contact form for Jordan

Hi Mariah,

I filled out most of the online registration for Jordan for the winter youth leadership nights, but I wasn’t sure how you wanted the emergency contact section done, so I’m sending the details here.

If something happens during meetings or outings, please list me as his primary emergency contact and note the relationship as "Mother/Guardian." I’m usually the one who can leave work fastest to pick him up or talk to medical staff. Carlos’s schedule at the logistics company is all over the place, so it’s harder for him to get to the phone.

For a secondary contact, you can put my mom, Rosa Delgado, and mark the relationship as "Grandmother." She lives in Peoria and is retired, so she’s often available if I’m stuck at the clinic.

One thing to flag: back in May 2024, during his 30‑minute dinner break at Fry’s on W Peoria Ave, Jordan got lightheaded and almost threw up after eating a super spicy habanero burrito. His manager, Luis, even wrote him up because he needed an extra 15 minutes in the bathroom to calm down. Since then I’ve told him to avoid really spicy foods before shifts or events, so for coalition activities it’s better if he sticks to mild options.

Let me know if you need anything else for the emergency paperwork.

Thank you,
Elena Rivera

metadata.subject: Emergency contact form for Jordan

artifacts.content:
Title: Coalition Orientation & Emergency Forms
Date: 2025-01-15 17:30–19:30
Location: Glendale Community Center – Room B
Attendees: Jordan Michael Rivera; Elena Maria Rivera; Glendale Youth Coalition staff

Description:

New member orientation for Glendale Youth Coalition. Jordan needs to bring:
- School ID
- Insurance card copy
- Completed emergency contact sheet (paper or online)
- List of any allergies/special food issues

Elena plans to attend the first 30–45 minutes to help with forms. Staff said it’s important to clearly list who they should call first if there’s an emergency during evening activities or weekend service projects.

Notes for emergency section (Jordan + Elena talking in the car):
- Primary emergency contact: Elena, relationship: "Mother" (she leaves work at the clinic earlier than anyone else).
- Backup contact: Rosa Delgado, relationship: "Grandmother" (lives in Peoria, can drive over if needed).
- They might also ask for another adult relative; Elena suggested writing in "Uncle" and using Luis Delgado as a tertiary contact in case both she and Rosa are unavailable.

Food/spice note to mention verbally if there isn’t space on the form: in May 2024 Jordan got really sick and lightheaded from a super spicy habanero burrito during his 30‑minute dinner break at Fry’s on W Peoria Ave and ended up taking an extra 15 minutes in the bathroom; his manager Luis wasn’t thrilled and wrote him up. Since then Elena prefers he avoids super spicy food at events and sticks to mild options.

Reminder: Ask staff if they need signatures from BOTH parents or just one legal guardian on the emergency paperwork.

From: Coach Ramirez <aramirez@cactusviewhs.org>
To: jordan.rivera09@example.com
Cc: elena.rivera@example.com
Date: 2025-02-03 14:11
Subject: After-school intramurals & emergency contact clarification

Hi Jordan,

Thanks for turning in the intramural basketball packet. I noticed you left one line on the emergency contact section a little vague, so I want to make sure we have it correct before our first game.

Right now your form lists:

Primary emergency contact: "Elena M. Rivera – Mom"  
Secondary emergency contact: "Rosa Delgado – Grandma"  
Relationship for both looks fine, but the office likes the exact wording to match what’s in the district system, usually "Mother" and "Grandmother." They get picky about this if the nurse has to call someone.

If you’d like, you can also add a third contact. Some students put an aunt, uncle, or older cousin who lives nearby. Since your grandmother is already listed, another adult relative (for example, an uncle) might be helpful in case your mom is stuck at work and Rosa can’t drive at night.

Also, don’t forget to note any food issues or things that could make you sick during games or tournaments. You mentioned during tryouts that a super spicy habanero burrito once made you lightheaded on your 30‑minute dinner break at Fry’s on W Peoria Ave and you had to stay in the bathroom an extra 15 minutes, which got you written up by your manager. That’s the kind of thing the nurse likes to know so we can steer you toward milder options when we order team snacks.

Please bring the corrected form to practice tomorrow.

– Coach Ramirez

metadata.subject: After-school intramurals & emergency contact clarification

To: Ms. Alvarez <salvarez@glendalehealthcoalition.org>

Subject: Youth Health Coalition Permission Form Info

Hi Ms. Alvarez,

My mom asked me to email you the info you needed for the Youth Health Coalition signup since she’s still at work and it was easier for me to type it up.

I’m a student at Cactus View High School, and I’m in 10th grade this year (grade 10), in case you need that for the participant information section. I usually go by Jordan Rivera, but my full name is Jordan Michael Rivera.

About medical/diet stuff: I don’t have any serious food allergies, but I do have pretty bad seasonal allergies in the spring. During allergy season last April, our school nurse, Ms. Patel, actually wrote a note saying I should avoid outdoor lunch events on really high‑pollen days. At a campus club picnic my sneezing and runny nose got so bad that some teachers thought I might have COVID and they sent me home early, even though it was just allergies. For coalition activities, if you’re planning to eat outside on days like that, it would help if there’s an option to sit inside or at least away from the grass and trees while we eat.

I’m fine with regular physical activities like walking, games, or light sports, as long as I can keep a water bottle with me and maybe step inside if the pollen count is super high.

Let me know if you need anything else filled out or if my parents have to sign another form.

Thanks,
Jordan

artifacts.content:
From: Mr. Daniel Ortiz <dortiz@cactusviewhs.org>
To: jordan.rivera@student.cactusviewhs.org
Date: 2024-09-03 15:19
Subject: Welcome to 11th Grade U.S. History

Hi Jordan,

Welcome back! I’m glad to have you in my **11th-grade** U.S. History section this year. I know you’re juggling school with your cashier job at Fry’s, so I wanted to outline expectations early and make sure you’re clear on how your grade in this course will be calculated.

Your semester course grade will be based on:
- 40% unit tests and quizzes
- 30% projects (including the local history photo essay)
- 20% homework and classwork
- 10% participation

Since you’re in the upper grades now, colleges and scholarships will look closely at your history and English marks, so staying on top of assignments is important. Missing more than two major assignments can drop your course grade by a full letter. If work or family responsibilities get in the way, talk to me ahead of time so we can work something out.

Please bring your signed syllabus acknowledgement form by Friday. Also, reply to this email with the name of the elementary or middle school where you first got interested in history (if ever!) so I can get to know everyone a bit better.

Looking forward to a good year.

Best,
Mr. Ortiz
Social Studies Department
Cactus View High School

metadata.subject: Welcome to 11th Grade U.S. History

From: Glendale Youth League <info@gyleague.org>
To: jordan.rivera@gmail.com
Date: 2024-10-05 11:07
Subject: Incomplete Registration – Grade Level Needed

Hi Jordan,

Thanks for starting your registration for the Glendale Youth Basketball League’s fall session. We noticed that one section of your online form is incomplete: your current school grade level.

Right now, the system shows you selected "9th" and then changed it to "12th" before exiting. To place you on the correct team, we need your actual grade level (for example, 8th, 9th, 11th, etc.). Our high school division is split into two brackets: lower high school (grades 9–10) and upper high school (grades 11–12). Since you indicated that you attend Cactus View High School and mentioned in the comments that you’re taking U.S. History and Algebra II, it’s likely you fall into the **upper high school** bracket, but we can’t finalize anything without a confirmed grade.

Please log back into your account and update the "Current Grade" field so your registration can be processed. If you’re unsure, you can ask a parent or check your school portal. Once updated, we’ll assign you to a team that matches your grade and age group.

If we don’t receive the corrected grade information by October 12, your spot may be offered to someone on the waitlist.

Best,
Glendale Youth League Registration Team

Title: Zoom – Scholarship Info Session for 12th Graders (Jordan RSVP’d)
Date: 2026-09-18 06:00 PM–07:00 PM
Location: Zoom – link in email from cvhs-college@cactusviewhs.org
Attendees: Jordan Michael Rivera, CVHS College & Career Center Staff, other students

Description:

Jordan registered for this virtual information session targeted at **12th grade** students at Cactus View High School who are interested in applying for local and state scholarships. Although he is not yet a senior, he signed up after Ms. Patel from the College & Career Center mentioned that motivated underclassmen are welcome to listen in.

Session outline:

1. Overview of the scholarship search process for **12th graders**, including deadlines that typically fall in fall and winter of senior year.
2. Explanation of how GPA from 9th–11th grade heavily affects scholarship eligibility, and why juniors should start organizing documents early.
3. Tips for writing personal statements that explain family responsibilities, such as helping younger siblings with homework or contributing to household expenses through a part-time job.
4. Q&A about balancing senior-year course loads, work schedules, and application timelines.

Note for Jordan: You RSVP’d as “interested – planning ahead” and the form auto-tagged your grade level as **12th** because you selected the “Senior Scholarship Night” series instead of the general info series. Ms. Patel said it’s fine and that you can still attend to get a preview of what senior year will look like.

Reminder: Have your unofficial transcript handy, along with a list of activities (basketball, youth programs, Fry’s job) in case they walk through sample scholarship forms for **12th grade** students.

From: New Horizons Youth Coalition Forms <no-reply@nhycoalition.org>
To: Jordan Rivera <jordan.rivera@gmail.com>

Subject: Action Required – Incomplete Participant Phone Field

Hi Jordan,

Thank you for starting your New Horizons Youth Coalition registration. We noticed that the “participant phone” field on your form appears incomplete or may not be your direct contact number.

Currently, the system shows a number ending in 2841, which looks like a business line (possibly your parent’s office). For safety and communication purposes, we need a number where we can reach you or your household quickly by call or text. Examples of acceptable entries:

- Your personal cell (e.g., a number like 480-771-3046)
- A parent/guardian’s cell (e.g., 623-410-2397)
- A primary home or family line that is regularly answered

Please do NOT list:

- Your workplace number (for example, the Fry’s Food & Drug store line on W Peoria Ave)
- A relative’s phone who doesn’t live with you (such as your grandma in Peoria)
- A school office number

To update your participant phone, click the “Edit Registration” button in your portal and re‑enter the correct contact. While you’re there, double‑check the medical and dietary section. You mentioned a prior urgent care visit after a large dairy-heavy meal (queso and tres leches at a family carne asada) at Abrazo Arrowhead Campus; we want to be sure your lactose sensitivity is clearly noted so event meals can be planned appropriately.

Once you update the phone field and save, this notice will clear automatically.

Best,
New Horizons Registration System

Rivera (mother).” When I called that number this morning to verify, the person who answered said it was a wrong number.

For student safety, we need a working emergency contact phone that can be reached quickly during school hours. Ideally, this should be your main cell phone, but we can also add your work line or Jordan’s father’s number as backups. Right now, Jordan’s file only has the single number above plus the main household landline, which is listed as **(480) 555-3309**.

Please reply to this email with the best phone number to reach you in an emergency, and indicate whether it’s a cell, home, or work line. If you prefer, you can also call the health office directly at **(602) 555-1190** so we can update the record over the phone.

Until we receive an updated emergency contact phone, we’ll keep the current information on file, but I strongly recommend correcting it as soon as possible.

Thank you for your prompt attention,

Carla Jimenez, RN
Cactus View High School Health Office
Phone: (602) 555-1190
Fax: (602) 555-1191

metadata.subject: URGENT: Missing/Incorrect Emergency Contact Phone Number

From: Phoenix Youth Basketball League <info@phxyouthhoops.org>
To: Jordan Rivera <jordan.rivera09@student.cvusd.az.us>
Date: 2024-04-03 13:27
Subject: Incomplete Registration – Emergency Contact Phone Needed

Hi Jordan,

Thanks for signing up for the Phoenix Youth Basketball League spring session at the Glendale Rec Center. We’re excited to have you on the JV pickup roster.

Your online registration is almost complete, but we’re missing a valid emergency contact phone for your parent/guardian. On the form, the number in the “Emergency Contact Phone” field was entered as **(602) 555-0000**, which looks like a placeholder.

We need a real phone number that we can call if you get injured during a game or practice. You can list either your mom or dad as the primary emergency contact. If it helps, you can also add a secondary contact, such as a grandparent or other relative who lives nearby.

Example (do NOT copy this exactly):
Primary Contact: Elena Rivera (mother)
Emergency Contact Phone: **(480) 555-7812**
Secondary Contact: Rosa Delgado (grandmother)
Emergency Contact Phone: **(602) 555-3389**

Please reply to this email with the correct emergency contact phone(s), or log back into your account and update the “Parent/Guardian Emergency Contact” section. Until it’s updated, your registration will remain pending and you won’t be allowed to participate in games.

Let us know if you have any questions.

Best,

Coach Ramirez
Phoenix Youth Basketball League
League Phone: (602) 555-4421

Subject: Updated contact numbers for Jordan Rivera

Hi Ms. Gonzalez,

This is Elena, Jordan Rivera’s mom. I’m filling out the online health and emergency forms for the spring semester and wanted to make sure you have our most recent phone numbers on file in case anything happens during the school day.

My primary cell is 602-555-9034. That’s the best number to reach me during work hours, since I can usually step out to take a call from the school. Our home landline is 623-555-1182, but I’m not always near it, so please try my cell first.

If I don’t answer, you can call my husband, Carlos Rivera, at 480-555-6670. He works in Phoenix and sometimes can’t pick up right away because of his shift schedule, but he checks his voicemail regularly. For non-urgent matters, email is fine, but for anything medical or time-sensitive, please use the phone numbers above.

Jordan mentioned he sometimes gets sniffly from his allergies, especially in the spring, but as we’ve noted before, it’s usually controlled with over-the-counter cetirizine. No other health changes to report.

Please let me know if there’s a separate form where I need to list these numbers again, or if this email is enough for you to update his record.

Thank you for keeping an eye on him,

Elena Maria Rivera
Mother of Jordan M. Rivera
Cell: 602-555-9034
Home: 623-555-1182

From: Coach Ramirez <aramirez@cactusviewhs.org>
To: Jordan Rivera <jordan.rivera@student.cvusd.k12.az.us>
CC: Elena Rivera <elena.rivera.az@gmail.com>
Date: 2024-08-22 06:02 PM
Subject: Parent Contact Info for Intramural Basketball

Hi Jordan,

Glad to hear you’re interested in joining the after-school intramural basketball group. Before you can participate, I need a signed permission slip and at least one parent/guardian contact number in case of injury or if practice ends early.

On the form you turned in today, you wrote your mom’s name as “Elena M. Rivera” and listed a contact number of 623-555-4412. Please make sure this is her current phone and that she’s okay with being the primary contact. Some students prefer to list a different parent or relative who can usually pick them up more quickly.

If she would rather we call your dad, you can update the form with his name and phone instead. For example, if Carlos is the one who usually handles pick-ups, list him as the primary and your mom as secondary. If there’s a grandparent or uncle who lives nearby and can help in an emergency, you can add them as a backup with their number as well.

Once you confirm which number is best, I’ll update my roster before our first scrimmage next week.

Thanks,

Coach Alejandro Ramirez
Cactus View High School
PE & Intramural Sports
